title: California decriminalizes consensual same-sex acts
description: The Consenting Adult Sex Bill, written and pushed through the state legislature by Willie Brown, removed criminal penalties in California for private consensual sex between adults of the same sex.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: South Australia is first Australian state to decriminalize homosexuality
description: South Australia removed criminal penalties for private sexual acts between consenting adults of the same sex, ahead of every other Australian state.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Minneapolis passes first trans-inclusive civil rights law in the US
description: Minneapolis adopted civil rights legislation with protections covering transgender people, the first city in the United States to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Gay American Indians organization forms
description: Gay American Indians began work as the first liberation organization created by and for gay Native Americans.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First public lesbian coming-out in Japan
description: A group of twelve women became the first in Japan to identify publicly as lesbians, producing a single issue of the periodical Subarashi Onna (Wonderful Women).

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Leonard Matlovich comes out to challenge the US military ban
description: Air Force Technical Sergeant Leonard Matlovich deliberately disclosed his homosexuality while serving, the first US service member to out himself in order to contest the armed forces' exclusion of gay personnel.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Journal Gay Left starts publication in the UK
description: British writers launched Gay Left, a journal connecting gay liberation with socialist politics.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First US same-sex marriage licenses issued in Boulder County
description: County clerk Clela Rorex granted a marriage license to Dave McCord and Dave Zamora on 26 March, the first of six she issued to same-sex couples; the resulting marriages were voided later that year.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Maureen Colquhoun becomes the UK's first openly lesbian MP
description: Labour politician Maureen Colquhoun came out while sitting in Parliament, making her the first openly lesbian and first openly gay Member of Parliament in Britain.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Denmark equalizes the age of consent
description: Denmark set the same age of consent for same-sex and different-sex partners, removing the higher threshold that had applied to homosexual acts.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Australian Capital Territory decriminalizes homosexuality
description: The Australian Capital Territory ended criminal penalties for private sex between consenting adults of the same sex and equalized the age of consent at the same time.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Homosexual Law Reform Coalition and Gay Teachers Group form in Australia
description: Two Australian advocacy groups began work in 1976: the Homosexual Law Reform Coalition, campaigning to change criminal law, and the Gay Teachers Group, organizing gay and lesbian educators.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Association of Homosexual Liberation forms in Japan
description: Activists in Japan established the Association of Homosexual Liberation, an early gay rights group in the country.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Robert Grant launches Christian Voice against gay rights
description: Robert Grant created the Christian Voice organization to take his campaign against homosexual rights to a national audience in the United States. Some accounts date the group's formal founding to 1978.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Allan Spear re-elected to the Minnesota Senate after coming out
description: State senator Allan Spear, who had come out publicly in 1974, won re-election in Minnesota, showing that an openly gay legislator could hold his seat.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Tom Gallagher becomes first US Foreign Service officer to come out
description: Diplomat Tom Gallagher publicly acknowledged he was gay, a first for the US Foreign Service; he then resigned because the disclosure made a security clearance unattainable.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: The Fancy Dancer brings gay small-town life to bestseller lists
description: Patricia Nell Warren's third novel, The Fancy Dancer, became the first bestseller centered on a gay priest and on gay life in a small town.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: US Episcopal Church affirms gay people as full members
description: The Episcopal Church in the United States adopted a resolution declaring that homosexual persons are children of God with a full claim on the church's love, acceptance and pastoral care.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Croatia decriminalizes homosexuality
description: The Yugoslav republic of Croatia removed consensual same-sex acts between adults from its criminal code.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Montenegro decriminalizes homosexuality
description: The Yugoslav republic of Montenegro removed consensual same-sex acts between adults from its criminal code.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Slovenia decriminalizes homosexuality
description: The Yugoslav republic of Slovenia removed consensual same-sex acts between adults from its criminal code.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Vojvodina decriminalizes homosexuality
description: Vojvodina, an autonomous province within the Yugoslav republic of Serbia, ended criminal penalties for consensual same-sex acts between adults.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Dade County rights ordinance repealed after Anita Bryant campaign
description: Dade County, Florida enacted a human rights ordinance covering sexual orientation, then voters repealed it within the year following the anti-gay Save Our Children campaign fronted by Anita Bryant.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Quebec bans discrimination based on sexual orientation
description: Quebec added sexual orientation to its human rights charter, becoming the first jurisdiction larger than a city or county to bar such discrimination in both the public and private sectors.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Boston-Boise Committee and National Center for Lesbian Rights form in US
description: The Boston-Boise Committee organized after a police raid on a house near Boston angered the local gay community, while the National Center for Lesbian Rights began providing legal advocacy for lesbians.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Platonica Club and Front Runners form in Japan
description: Two Japanese groups, the Platonica Club and Front Runners, took shape in 1977, adding to the country's early gay organizing.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Harvey Milk elected San Francisco supervisor
description: Harvey Milk won a seat on the San Francisco Board of Supervisors, the first openly gay or lesbian candidate elected to office in California and only the seventh openly gay or lesbian elected official in the country.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Jeffrey Weeks publishes Coming Out
description: Welsh historian Jeffrey Weeks published Coming Out, an early scholarly history of homosexual politics in Britain.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Gaysweek debuts as New York City's first mainstream gay weekly
description: The first issue of Gaysweek appeared, giving New York City a mainstream weekly publication aimed at gay readers.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Anne Holmes ordained as first openly lesbian UCC minister
description: The United Church of Christ ordained Anne Holmes, the first openly lesbian minister in the denomination.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Ellen Barrett ordained as first openly lesbian Episcopal priest
description: The Episcopal Church of the United States ordained Ellen Barrett to the priesthood for the Diocese of New York, its first priest to be openly lesbian.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Angel Dance published as first American lesbian mystery novel
description: Mary F. Beal's Angel Dance reached print as the first lesbian mystery novel published in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Shakuntala Devi publishes first Indian study of homosexuality
description: Shakuntala Devi released the first study of homosexuality to appear in India.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: San Francisco hosts the world's first gay film festival
description: The world's first gay film festival took place in San Francisco, where the Mariposa Film Group premiered Word Is Out: Stories of Some of Our Lives at the Castro Theater — the first feature-length documentary about gay identity made by gay and lesbian filmmakers.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Beth Chayim Chadashim becomes first LGBT synagogue with its own building
description: The Los Angeles congregation Beth Chayim Chadashim acquired its own building, a first for any synagogue serving LGBT Jews.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Gay rights discussed at the White House for the first time
description: Frank Kameny, the National Gay Task Force and about a dozen other activists briefed presidential aide Midge Costanza on needed changes to federal law and policy — the first official White House discussion of gay rights.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Spain's first gay pride march takes place in Barcelona
description: During the transition to democracy, demonstrators marched along La Rambla in Spain's first gay pride demonstration; police responded with violence, including rubber bullets.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Sweden equalizes the age of consent
description: Sweden brought the age of consent for same-sex relations into line with the age applying to heterosexual relations.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Samois, earliest known lesbian-feminist BDSM group, forms in San Francisco
description: Samois organized in San Francisco as the earliest known lesbian-feminist BDSM group; members including Patrick Califia and Gayle Rubin were among the first advocates of what became known as sex-positive feminism.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: International Lesbian and Gay Association founded
description: Activists from several countries created the International Lesbian and Gay Association (ILGA) at a meeting in Coventry, England, forming a worldwide federation of lesbian and gay organizations.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Harvey Milk and George Moscone assassinated
description: Former supervisor Dan White shot and killed San Francisco Supervisor Harvey Milk and Mayor George Moscone at City Hall, taking the life of the most prominent openly gay elected official in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First Sydney Gay and Lesbian Mardi Gras
description: About two thousand people joined the first Mardi Gras march in Sydney; police arrested 53 participants and seriously beat some of them.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Rainbow flag flown as a gay pride symbol for the first time
description: The rainbow flag made its debut as a symbol of gay pride at San Francisco's Gay Freedom Day celebration, beginning its spread as the movement's emblem worldwide.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Robin Tyler becomes first out lesbian on US national television
description: Comedian Robin Tyler appeared as an out lesbian on a Showtime special hosted by Phyllis Diller, then released Always a Bridesmaid, Never a Groom, the first comedy album by an openly lesbian performer.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: San Francisco Gay Freedom Day Marching Band formed
description: Jon Reed Sims formed the San Francisco Gay Freedom Day Marching Band and Twirling Corp — later the San Francisco Lesbian/Gay Freedom Band — the first openly gay musical group anywhere.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: San Francisco runs first US recruitment drive for gay police officers
description: San Francisco became the first American city to actively recruit gay police officers, drawing more than 350 applications.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Allen Bennett becomes first openly gay rabbi in the US
description: Allen Bennett came out publicly, making him the first rabbi in the United States known to be openly gay.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Cuba decriminalizes homosexuality
description: Cuba removed private consensual same-sex relations between adults from its penal code.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Spain decriminalizes homosexuality
description: Spain removed same-sex relations from the scope of its social-danger law, ending the criminal treatment of homosexuality carried over from the Franco era.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Sweden becomes first country to drop homosexuality as an illness
description: After Swedes phoned in sick to work claiming to suffer from homosexuality and activists occupied the head office of the National Board of Health and Welfare, the agency removed homosexuality from its register of illnesses — the first country in the world to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Sisters of Perpetual Indulgence appear in San Francisco
description: The Sisters of Perpetual Indulgence, an order of queer nuns combining street theater with activism, made their first public appearance in San Francisco on Easter Sunday.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Japan Gay Center established
description: The Japan Gay Center was set up in 1979 as an early organizing hub for gay men in the country.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First National March on Washington for Lesbian and Gay Rights
description: The first national demonstration for lesbian and gay rights in the United States, the National March on Washington for Lesbian and Gay Rights, drew marchers to the capital.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: White Night riots in San Francisco
description: After Dan White received a manslaughter verdict rather than a murder conviction for killing Harvey Milk and George Moscone, protests in San Francisco escalated into rioting at City Hall, followed by a police raid on the Castro district.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Harry Hay calls first Radical Faerie gathering
description: Harry Hay issued the first call for a Radical Faerie gathering, held in Arizona, starting a countercultural spiritual movement of gay men.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Esta Noche opens as San Francisco's first gay Latino bar
description: Esta Noche opened at 16th and Mission Streets, giving San Francisco its first bar serving gay Latinos.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First known gay couple attends a US high school prom
description: Grady Quinn and Randy Rohl went together to the Lincoln High School prom in Sioux Falls, South Dakota, the first known same-sex couple to attend a high school prom.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Stephen Lachs appointed as first openly gay judge in the US
description: California appointed Stephen Lachs to the bench, making him the first openly gay judge in the United States and, as far as is documented, in the world.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First picture book with lesbian characters published
description: Jane Severance's When Megan Went Away, illustrated by Tea Schook, appeared as the first children's picture book to include LGBT characters, specifically lesbian ones — a distinction sometimes wrongly credited to Heather Has Two Mommies (1989).

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Scotland decriminalizes homosexuality
description: Legislation passed in 1980 ended criminal penalties in Scotland for private consensual sex between men aged 21 and over; it took effect early the following year.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)
